name: Crab in Ginger Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp (5 mL) instant chicken bouillon granules
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse crabs with water.
Gently pull away the round hard shell on top. With a small sharp knife, gently cut away the gray spongy tissue and discard.
Rinse crabs with water.
2. Cut off claws and legs. Pound claws lightly with the back of a cleaver to break the shell. Chop down the center of the crabs to cut the body in half. Cut each half crosswise into 3 pieces.
3. Cut onions into 1-inch (2.5 cm) pieces. Remove seeds from pepper. Cut pepper into thin strips. Pare ginger root. Cut ginger into thin slices, then cut the slices into very thin strips.
4. Combine 1/2 cup (125 mL) of water, the sherry, sugar, bouillon, and soy sauce in a bowl. Combine the remaining 1/4 cup (60 mL) water and cornstarch in another bowl. Blend well.
5. Heat vegetable and sesame oils in a wok over medium heat. Stir-fry ginger in the oils for 1 minute. Add cut crabs. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
6. Add sherry mixture and pepper to crab. Stir-fry over high heat until liquid boils. Reduce heat to low. Simmer covered for 4 minutes. Uncover and stir in cornstarch mixture. Cook until sauce thickens. Add onions. Cook and stir for 1 minute.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
